items.title: 'The Odyssey' is cleaning up for IMAX. Expect the momentum to continue
items.description: Nolan's "The Odyssey" is racking up box office dollars for IMAX and fueling investor confidence that the company will reach record ticket sales in 2026.

items.title: As Honda CR-V leads U.S. sales, automaker teases new American-built pickup truck
items.description: The future of the Ridgeline midsize pickup truck had been in flux, and the automaker said there will be a temporary production stoppage for the truck.

items.title: Congress splits on war powers resolutions to force Trump to abandon Iran war
items.description: Trump would be nearly certain to veto any measure to curtail his ability to wage war with Iran.

items.title: Shortsighted stock market can no longer brush off war: 'It's too hard to ignore $100 oil'
items.description: After staying flat as the U.S.-Iran war heated up again, equities tumbled on Thursday after oil prices crossed $100 per barrel.

items.title: Google slapped with $1 billion fine under landmark EU digital law
items.description: European regulators fined Google $1 billion, alleging the company gives preferential treatment to its own services.

items.title: Google Cloud CEO Kurian says customers are spending 50% more as segment blows away expectations
items.description: Google Cloud CEO Thomas Kurian told CNBC on Thursday that the company's existing customers are spending "roughly 50% more" than they've already committed.

items.title: Amazon and Microsoft pivot cloud gaming strategies to target different players
items.description: Amazon is bringing Luna to the Prime Video streaming service, while Microsoft will test an ad-supported streaming of titles that people own.

items.title: House lawmakers grill former JPMorgan executive Jes Staley over Epstein ties
items.description: House oversight Chair James Comer said Jes Staley defended Jeffrey Epstein and encouraged JPMorgan to retain him despite internal red flags.

items.title: Tesla falls 13%, Alphabet sinks 7% as AI spending concerns spook investors
items.description: Both Tesla and Alphabet signaled higher spending as they invest in artificial intelligence.

items.title: 10-year Treasury yield rises to highest since January 2025 as surging oil rekindles inflation fear
items.description: U.S. Treasury yields climbed on Wednesday, following oil prices higher, as traders also reacted to a slump in weekly claims for unemployment insurance.

items.title: We're ready to move on from Dover after the industrial conglomerate's weak quarter
items.description: We're downgrading the stock because our money can be put to better use elsewhere.

items.title: Albertsons stock plunges as it says weaker grocery spending will cut into sales and earnings
items.description: Shares of Albertsons sank Thursday after the grocer reported softness in its core business and lowered its full-year outlook as a result.

items.title: Alphabet and Tesla test Wall Street's patience as AI spending overshadows growth
items.description: Shares of Alphabet and Tesla dipped after the companies reported massive spending increases in their quarterly earnings reports.

items.title: American Airlines stock tumbles 8% as fuel spike further postpones turnaround
items.description: American Airlines further cut its 2026 earnings outlook, citing higher fuel costs.

items.title: Comcast earnings highlight NBCUniversal strength ahead of planned split
items.description: Comcast said earlier this year it would split the cable businesses from the media business, creating two separate publicly traded companies.

items.title: Cyclospora outbreak tests RFK Jr.'s promise to overhaul food system, rebuild trust in CDC
items.description: Cyclospora presents a test for HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.'s promise to overhaul the food system and restore confidence in the agencies overseeing it.

items.title: IRS chief Frank Bisignano may have misled Congress, Democrats say
items.description: The IRS alone hemorrhaged more than 31,000 employees as of January in President Trump's government cuts, according to the agency's inspector general.

items.title: Tech reporter Joanna Stern asked AI to do 'almost everything' for a year—the No. 1 tool she'll keep using
items.description: "I Am Not a Robot" author Joanna Stern integrated dozens of AI applications into her life over the course of a year. She says this one has stuck with her.

items.title: Eli Lilly says it will file for approval of next-generation obesity drug in 2027 as it clears two more trials
items.description: There are now positive results from five late-stage trials on retatrutide, which Lilly is positioning as the next pillar of its obesity portfolio.

items.title: Brent crude tops $100 a barrel. How the next stop could be $120
items.description: Goldman Sachs warns on oil prices and so called tank bottoms ( and loves a new natural gas-related name).
